channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Dems struggle to name the leader of the Democratic Party
item.description: Top voices in the Democratic Party, as well as average Americans, have struggled to answer who the party's leader is when asked in interviews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah bans LGBTQ+ pride flags, MAGA flags, other unapproved flags in government buildings, schools
item.description: Utah became the first state to prohibit LGBTQ+ pride flags from being displayed at government buildings and schools after the governor declined to reject the measure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military review of fitness standards will find array of tests, but higher requirements for combat
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s decision to review military standards on combat and physical fitness and appearance opens a Pandora’s box of the widely different rules among the services.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Critics see Trump attacks on the 'Black Smithsonian' as an effort to sanitize racism in US history
item.description: Civil rights advocates, historians and Black political leaders are sharply rebuking President Donald Trump for his order targeting the Smithsonian Institution.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump moved Thursday to end collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No timetable from Texas governor on filling late Democratic congressman's vacant seat
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The race to fill a Texas congressional seat has candidates but no election date more than three weeks after Rep. Sylvester Turner’s death left a vacancy in a stronghold for Democrats, who are eager to cut into Republicans’ narrow U.S. House majority.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas hospital treating children with vitamin A toxicity: Reports
item.description: The CDC notes that it is not a substitute for vaccination, and warns that overusing Vitamin A can lead to toxicity.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: US and Lithuanian forces struggle to drain swamp to recover 4 soldiers in submerged armored vehicle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. and Lithuanian troops and authorities are struggling to drain a swampy area in Lithuania in an ongoing effort to recover four American Army soldiers who went missing three days ago.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells automakers not to raise prices over tariff: Official
item.description: President Donald Trump believes costs associated with the auto tariff should not be passed onto the American people
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota doctor: Hospitals in ‘existential crisis’ and potential Medicaid cuts won’t help
item.description: Dr. Rahul Koranne, president and CEO of the Minnesota Hospital Association, joined Cathy Wurzer to talk about what recent job loss and federal volatility mean for the health care of all Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump team revokes $11 billion in funding for addiction, mental health care
item.description: The Trump administration says it hopes to save $11.4 billion by freezing and revoking COVID-era grants. Addiction experts say clawing back the federal funding is risky and could put patients at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Foragers want a seat at the rulemaking table as DNR mulls mushroom, berry bag limits
item.description: The DNR is considering bag limits on how much food people can take from state-managed lands. That gallon limit is ringing alarm bells for foraging groups like the Minnesota Mycological Society.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5
